We find that: (a) LLMs are susceptible to inferring causal relations from the order of two entity mentions in text (e.g. X mentioned before Y implies X causes Y); (b) if the order is randomized, LLMs still suffer from the post hoc fallacy, i.e. X occurs before Y (temporal relation) implies X causes Y. We also find that while LLMs can correctly deduce the absence of causal relations from temporal and spatial relations, they have difficulty inferring causal relations from counterfactuals, questioning their understanding of causality.
摘要：最近的工作表明，通过提示可以有效地从LLMS中提取因果事实，这有助于为因果推理任务创建因果关系图。然而，目前尚不清楚这种成功是否仅限于模型可以记忆的预训练数据中明确提到的因果事实。因此，这项工作研究：LLMS能否从文本中的其他关系数据中推断出因果关系？为了弄清记忆的因果事实与推断的因果关系的作用，我们对包含时间、空间和反事实关系的合成数据进行了微调，并测量了LLM是否能够推断因果关系。我们发现：(A)LLM容易从语篇中两个实体提及的顺序来推断因果关系(例如，在Y之前提到的X意味着X导致Y)；(B)如果顺序是随机的，LLM仍然存在后随机谬误，即X出现在Y(时间关系)意味着X导致Y之前。我们还发现，尽管LLM可以正确地从时间和空间关系中推断因果关系的缺失，但他们很难从反事实中推断因果关系，质疑他们对因果关系的理解。

[NLP-118] A dual task learning approach to fine-tune a multilingual semantic speech encoder for Spoken Language Understanding
[NLP-118] 双任务学习方法微调多语言语义语音编码器以实现口语理解

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12141
作者: Gaëlle Laperrière,Sahar Ghannay,Bassam Jabaian,Yannick Estève
关键词: Spoken Language Understanding, gradually replacing conventional, replacing conventional approaches, efficiently represent speech, Language Understanding
中文关键词: 口语理解，逐渐取代传统，取代传统方法，有效地表示语音，语言理解
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Sound (cs.SD); Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)
备注: In Proceedings of Interspeech 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Self-Supervised Learning is vastly used to efficiently represent speech for Spoken Language Understanding, gradually replacing conventional approaches. Meanwhile, textual SSL models are proposed to encode language-agnostic semantics. SAMU-XLSR framework employed this semantic information to enrich multilingual speech representations. A recent study investigated SAMU-XLSR in-domain semantic enrichment by specializing it on downstream transcriptions, leading to state-of-the-art results on a challenging SLU task. This study’s interest lies in the loss of multilingual performances and lack of specific-semantics training induced by such specialization in close languages without any SLU implication. We also consider SAMU-XLSR’s loss of initial cross-lingual abilities due to a separate SLU fine-tuning. Therefore, this paper proposes a dual task learning approach to improve SAMU-XLSR semantic enrichment while considering distant languages for multilingual and language portability experiments.
摘要：在口语理解中，自我监督学习被广泛用于有效地表示语音，逐渐取代传统的方法。同时，提出了文本SSL模型来编码与语言无关的语义。SAMU-XLSR框架利用这些语义信息来丰富多语言的语音表示。最近的一项研究通过专门研究下游转录来研究SAMU-XLSR域内语义丰富，导致了一项具有挑战性的SLU任务的最新结果。这项研究的兴趣在于，在没有任何SLU含义的封闭语言中，这种专业化导致了多语种表现的损失和特定语义训练的缺乏。我们还考虑了SAMU-XLSR由于单独的SLU微调而失去了初始的跨语言能力。因此，本文提出了一种双任务学习方法来改进SAMU-XLSR的语义丰富，同时考虑了远程语言的多语言和语言可移植性实验。

[NLP-119] Bias in Text Embedding Models
[NLP-119] 文本嵌入模型中的偏差

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12138
作者: Vasyl Rakivnenko,Nestor Maslej,Jessica Cervi,Volodymyr Zhukov
关键词: text embedding models, Text embedding, embedding models, popular text embedding, embedding
中文关键词: 文本嵌入模型，文本嵌入，嵌入模型，流行的文本嵌入，嵌入
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Text embedding is becoming an increasingly popular AI methodology, especially among businesses, yet the potential of text embedding models to be biased is not well understood. This paper examines the degree to which a selection of popular text embedding models are biased, particularly along gendered dimensions. More specifically, this paper studies the degree to which these models associate a list of given professions with gendered terms. The analysis reveals that text embedding models are prone to gendered biases but in varying ways. Although there are certain inter-model commonalities, for instance, greater association of professions like nurse, homemaker, and socialite with female identifiers, and greater association of professions like CEO, manager, and boss with male identifiers, not all models make the same gendered associations for each occupation. Furthermore, the magnitude and directionality of bias can also vary on a model-by-model basis and depend on the particular words models are prompted with. This paper demonstrates that gender bias afflicts text embedding models and suggests that businesses using this technology need to be mindful of the specific dimensions of this problem.
摘要：文本嵌入正成为一种越来越受欢迎的人工智能方法论，特别是在企业中，然而文本嵌入模型的潜在偏见还没有被很好地理解。本文考察了一些流行的文本嵌入模型的偏颇程度，特别是在性别维度上。更具体地说，本文研究了这些模型将给定职业列表与性别术语相关联的程度。分析表明，文本嵌入模式容易产生性别偏见，但存在不同程度的性别偏见。尽管模特之间存在某些共性，例如，护士、家庭主妇和社会名流等职业与女性身份标识的关联度更高，首席执行官、经理和老板等职业与男性身份标识的关联度更大，但并不是所有的模特都会对每个职业进行同样的性别关联。此外，偏差的大小和方向性也可能因模型的不同而不同，并取决于提示模型的特定词语。本文论证了性别偏见对文本嵌入模型的影响，并建议使用这种技术的企业需要注意这个问题的特定维度。

[NLP-120] Gram2Vec: An Interpretable Document Vectorizer
[NLP-120] Gram 2Vec：可解释的文档载体器

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12131
作者: Peter Zeng,Eric Sclafani,Owen Rambow
关键词: grammatical style embedding, style embedding algorithm, higher dimensional space, normalized relative frequencies, grammatical features present
中文关键词: 语法风格嵌入、风格嵌入算法、更高维度空间、规范化相对频率、存在的语法特征
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 6 pages, 2 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We present Gram2Vec, a grammatical style embedding algorithm that embeds documents into a higher dimensional space by extracting the normalized relative frequencies of grammatical features present in the text. Compared to neural approaches, Gram2Vec offers inherent interpretability based on how the feature vectors are generated. In our demo, we present a way to visualize a mapping of authors to documents based on their Gram2Vec vectors and highlight the ability to drop or add features to view which authors make certain linguistic choices. Next, we use authorship attribution as an application to show how Gram2Vec can explain why a document is attributed to a certain author, using cosine similarities between the Gram2Vec feature vectors to calculate the distances between candidate documents and a query document.
摘要：我们提出了Gram 2Vec，这是一种语法风格嵌入算法，通过提取文本中语法特征的规范化相对频率来将文档嵌入到更高维度的空间中。与神经方法相比，Gram 2Vec根据特征载体的生成方式提供了固有的解释性。在我们的演示中，我们提供了一种基于Gram2Vec载体可视化作者到文档的映射的方法，并强调了删除或添加特征以查看哪些作者做出某些语言选择的能力。接下来，我们使用作者身份归属作为应用程序来展示Gram2Vec如何解释文档归因于某个作者的原因，使用Gram2Vec特征载体之间的cos相似性来计算候选文档和查询文档之间的距离。

[NLP-121] AI “News” Content Farms Are Easy to Make and Hard to Detect: A Case Study in Italian
[NLP-121] 人工智能“新闻”内容农场易于制作且难以检测：意大利案例研究

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12128
作者: Giovanni Puccetti,Anna Rogers,Chiara Alzetta,Felice Dell’Orletta,Andrea Esuli
关键词: Large Language Models, Language Models, Models, generate synthetic text, content farm
中文关键词: 大型语言模型，语言模型，模型，生成合成文本，内容场
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used as “content farm” models (CFMs), to generate synthetic text that could pass for real news articles. This is already happening even for languages that do not have high-quality monolingual LLMs. We show that fine-tuning Llama (v1), mostly trained on English, on as little as 40K Italian news articles, is sufficient for producing news-like texts that native speakers of Italian struggle to identify as synthetic. We investigate three LLMs and three methods of detecting synthetic texts (log-likelihood, DetectGPT, and supervised classification), finding that they all perform better than human raters, but they are all impractical in the real world (requiring either access to token likelihood information or a large dataset of CFM texts). We also explore the possibility of creating a proxy CFM: an LLM fine-tuned on a similar dataset to one used by the real “content farm”. We find that even a small amount of fine-tuning data suffices for creating a successful detector, but we need to know which base LLM is used, which is a major challenge. Our results suggest that there are currently no practical methods for detecting synthetic news-like texts ‘in the wild’, while generating them is too easy. We highlight the urgency of more NLP research on this problem. Subjects: Computation and Language (cs.CL) Cite as: arXiv:2406.12128 [cs.CL] (or arXiv:2406.12128v1 [cs.CL] for this version)
摘要：大型语言模型(LLM)越来越多地被用作“内容农场”模型(CFM)，以生成可以被认为是真实新闻文章的合成文本。即使对于没有高质量的单语LLM的语言来说，这种情况也已经发生了。我们表明，微调Llama(V1)，主要是对英语进行培训，对仅4万篇意大利新闻文章进行微调，足以产生以意大利语为母语的人难以识别为合成的新闻类文本。我们研究了三种LLM和三种检测合成文本的方法(对数似然、DetectGPT和监督分类)，发现它们都比人类评分者表现得更好，但在现实世界中都是不切实际的(需要访问标记似然信息或CFM文本的大数据集)。我们还探索了创建代理CFM的可能性：在与真正的“内容场”使用的数据集类似的数据集上微调的LLM。我们发现，即使是少量的微调数据也足以创建一个成功的探测器，但我们需要知道使用的是哪个基本LLM，这是一个重大挑战。我们的结果表明，目前还没有实用的方法来检测合成的类似新闻的文本，而生成它们太容易了。我们强调了对这一问题进行更多自然语言处理研究的紧迫性。科目：计算和语言(cs.CL)引用为：arxiv：2406.12128cs.CL

[NLP-122] Efficient Sequential Decision Making with Large Language Models
[NLP-122] 使用大型语言模型进行高效顺序决策

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12125
作者: Dingyang Chen,Qi Zhang,Yinglun Zhu
关键词: large language models, decision making, focuses on extending, extending the success, success of large
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、决策制定、专注于扩展、扩展成功、大型的成功
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This paper focuses on extending the success of large language models (LLMs) to sequential decision making. Existing efforts either (i) re-train or finetune LLMs for decision making, or (ii) design prompts for pretrained LLMs. The former approach suffers from the computational burden of gradient updates, and the latter approach does not show promising results. In this paper, we propose a new approach that leverages online model selection algorithms to efficiently incorporate LLMs agents into sequential decision making. Statistically, our approach significantly outperforms both traditional decision making algorithms and vanilla LLM agents. Computationally, our approach avoids the need for expensive gradient updates of LLMs, and throughout the decision making process, it requires only a small number of LLM calls. We conduct extensive experiments to verify the effectiveness of our proposed approach. As an example, on a large-scale Amazon dataset, our approach achieves more than a 6 x performance gain over baselines while calling LLMs in only 1.5 % of the time steps.
摘要：本文将大语言模型的成功推广到序贯决策中。现有的工作包括：(I)为决策重新培训或微调LLM，或(Ii)为预先培训的LLM提供设计提示。前一种方法存在梯度更新的计算负担，而后一种方法的结果并不令人满意。在本文中，我们提出了一种新的方法，该方法利用在线模型选择算法来有效地将LLMS代理整合到序列决策中。从统计上看，我们的方法比传统的决策算法和普通的LLM代理都要好得多。在计算上，我们的方法避免了对LLM进行昂贵的梯度更新，并且在整个决策过程中，它只需要少量的LLM调用。我们进行了大量的实验来验证我们所提出的方法的有效性。例如，在一个大规模的Amazon数据集上，我们的方法在调用LLM的时间步长仅为1.5%的情况下，获得了超过基线6倍的性能提升。

[NLP-123] Decoding the Narratives: Analyzing Personal Drug Experiences Shared on Reddit
[NLP-123] 解码叙事：分析Reddit上分享的个人吸毒经历

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12117
作者: Layla Bouzoubaa,Elham Aghakhani,Max Song,Minh Trinh,Rezvaneh Rezapour
关键词: drug-related subreddits serve, drug-related subreddits, subreddits serve, serve as safe, safe spaces
中文关键词: 与毒品相关的子Reddit服务，与毒品相关的子Reddit服务，子Reddit服务，充当安全、安全的空间
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Findings of the Association for Computational Linguistics: ACL 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Online communities such as drug-related subreddits serve as safe spaces for people who use drugs (PWUD), fostering discussions on substance use experiences, harm reduction, and addiction recovery. Users’ shared narratives on these forums provide insights into the likelihood of developing a substance use disorder (SUD) and recovery potential. Our study aims to develop a multi-level, multi-label classification model to analyze online user-generated texts about substance use experiences. For this purpose, we first introduce a novel taxonomy to assess the nature of posts, including their intended connections (Inquisition or Disclosure), subjects (e.g., Recovery, Dependency), and specific objectives (e.g., Relapse, Quality, Safety). Using various multi-label classification algorithms on a set of annotated data, we show that GPT-4, when prompted with instructions, definitions, and examples, outperformed all other models. We apply this model to label an additional 1,000 posts and analyze the categories of linguistic expression used within posts in each class. Our analysis shows that topics such as Safety, Combination of Substances, and Mental Health see more disclosure, while discussions about physiological Effects focus on harm reduction. Our work enriches the understanding of PWUD’s experiences and informs the broader knowledge base on SUD and drug use.
摘要：在线社区，如与毒品相关的子社区，为吸毒者(PWUD)提供了安全空间，促进了关于药物使用经验、减少危害和成瘾康复的讨论。用户在这些论坛上的共享叙述提供了对发展成物质使用障碍(SUD)和康复潜力的可能性的见解。我们的研究旨在开发一个多层次、多标签的分类模型来分析在线用户生成的关于物质使用经验的文本。为此，我们首先引入了一种新的分类来评估职位的性质，包括它们的预期联系(宗教调查或披露)、主题(例如，恢复、依赖)和特定目标(例如，复发、质量、安全)。在一组带注释的数据上使用不同的多标签分类算法，我们证明了GPT-4在得到指令、定义和示例的提示时，比所有其他模型都要好。我们应用这个模型来标注另外1000个帖子，并分析每个类别的帖子中使用的语言表达的类别。我们的分析表明，诸如安全、物质组合和精神健康等话题的披露更多，而关于生理影响的讨论集中在减少伤害上。我们的工作丰富了对PWUD经验的理解，并为更广泛的关于SUD和药物使用的知识提供了信息。

[NLP-124] Enhancing Text Classification through LLM-Driven Active Learning and Human Annotation
[NLP-124] 通过LLM驱动的主动学习和人类注释增强文本分类

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12114
作者: Hamidreza Rouzegar,Masoud Makrehchi
关键词: creating training data, critical issue, context of text, financial burden, exercises for creating
中文关键词: 创建培训数据、关键问题、文本上下文、财务负担、创建练习
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: Publisher: Association for Computational Linguistics URL: this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In the context of text classification, the financial burden of annotation exercises for creating training data is a critical issue. Active learning techniques, particularly those rooted in uncertainty sampling, offer a cost-effective solution by pinpointing the most instructive samples for manual annotation. Similarly, Large Language Models (LLMs) such as GPT-3.5 provide an alternative for automated annotation but come with concerns regarding their reliability. This study introduces a novel methodology that integrates human annotators and LLMs within an Active Learning framework. We conducted evaluations on three public datasets. IMDB for sentiment analysis, a Fake News dataset for authenticity discernment, and a Movie Genres dataset for multi-label classification.The proposed framework integrates human annotation with the output of LLMs, depending on the model uncertainty levels. This strategy achieves an optimal balance between cost efficiency and classification performance. The empirical results show a substantial decrease in the costs associated with data annotation while either maintaining or improving model accuracy.
摘要：在文本分类的背景下，创建训练数据的注释练习的经济负担是一个关键问题。主动学习技术，特别是那些植根于不确定性采样的技术，通过准确地找到最有指导意义的样本进行手动注释，提供了一种经济高效的解决方案。同样，大型语言模型(LLM)，如GPT-3.5，为自动标注提供了一种替代方案，但也带来了对其可靠性的担忧。这项研究介绍了一种新的方法，它将人工注释员和LLMS整合到一个主动学习框架中。我们对三个公共数据集进行了评估。IMDb用于情感分析，假新闻数据集用于真实性识别，电影流派数据集用于多标签分类，该框架根据模型的不确定性程度将人工标注与LLMS的输出相结合。该策略实现了成本效率和分类性能之间的最佳平衡。实证结果表明，在保持或提高模型精度的同时，与数据注释相关的成本显著降低。

[NLP-125] Can LLMs Learn Macroeconomic Narratives from Social Media?
[NLP-125] 法学硕士可以从社交媒体中学习宏观经济叙事吗？

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12109
作者: Almog Gueta,Amir Feder,Zorik Gekhman,Ariel Goldstein,Roi Reichart
关键词: affect public beliefs, influence economic fluctuations, study empirically tests, Natural Language Processing, public beliefs
中文关键词: 影响公众信仰，影响经济波动，研究实证测试，自然语言处理，公众信仰
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computational Engineering, Finance, and Science (cs.CE)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This study empirically tests the \textitNarrative Economics hypothesis, which posits that narratives (ideas that are spread virally and affect public beliefs) can influence economic fluctuations. We introduce two curated datasets containing posts from X (formerly Twitter) which capture economy-related narratives (Data will be shared upon paper acceptance). Employing Natural Language Processing (NLP) methods, we extract and summarize narratives from the tweets. We test their predictive power for \textitmacroeconomic forecasting by incorporating the tweets’ or the extracted narratives’ representations in downstream financial prediction tasks. Our work highlights the challenges in improving macroeconomic models with narrative data, paving the way for the research community to realistically address this important challenge. From a scientific perspective, our investigation offers valuable insights and NLP tools for narrative extraction and summarization using Large Language Models (LLMs), contributing to future research on the role of narratives in economics.
摘要：这项研究对叙事经济学假说进行了实证检验，该假说认为叙事(通过病毒传播并影响公众信仰的想法)可以影响经济波动。我们介绍了两个精选的数据集，其中包含来自X(前身为Twitter)的帖子，这些帖子捕捉了与经济相关的叙述(数据将在论文接受后共享)。我们使用自然语言处理(NLP)的方法，从推文中提取和总结叙述。我们通过在下游的金融预测任务中加入推文或提取的叙述的表征来测试它们对文本宏观经济预测的预测能力。我们的工作突出了使用叙述性数据改进宏观经济模型方面的挑战，为研究界切实解决这一重要挑战铺平了道路。从科学的角度来看，我们的研究为使用大型语言模型(LLM)提取和总结叙事提供了有价值的见解和自然语言处理工具，为未来关于叙事在经济学中的作用的研究做出了贡献。

[NLP-126] End-to-end Text-to-SQL Generation within an Analytics Insight Engine
[NLP-126] 分析洞察引擎中的端到端文本到SQL生成

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12104
作者: Karime Maamari,Amine Mhedhbi
关键词: Recent advancements, pushed database management, database management systems, Analytics Insight Engine, management systems
中文关键词: 最新进展、推送式数据库管理、数据库管理系统、分析洞察引擎、管理系统
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Databases (cs.DB); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recent advancements in Text-to-SQL have pushed database management systems towards greater democratization of data access. Today’s language models are at the core of these advancements. They enable impressive Text-to-SQL generation as experienced in the development of Distyl AI’s Analytics Insight Engine. Its early deployment with enterprise customers has highlighted three core challenges. First, data analysts expect support with authoring SQL queries of very high complexity. Second, requests are ad-hoc and, as such, require low latency. Finally, generation requires an understanding of domain-specific terminology and practices. The design and implementation of our Text-to-SQL generation pipeline, powered by large language models, tackles these challenges. The core tenants of our approach rely on external knowledge that we extract in a pre-processing phase, on retrieving the appropriate external knowledge at query generation time, and on decomposing SQL query generation following a hierarchical CTE-based structure. Finally, an adaptation framework leverages feedback to update the external knowledge, in turn improving query generation over time. We give an overview of our end-to-end approach and highlight the operators generating SQL during inference. Subjects: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Databases (cs.DB); Machine Learning (cs.LG) Cite as: arXiv:2406.12104 [cs.CL] (or arXiv:2406.12104v1 [cs.CL] for this version)
摘要：文本到SQL的最新进展已将数据库管理系统推向更大的数据访问民主化。今天的语言模型是这些进步的核心。它们能够生成令人印象深刻的文本到SQL，就像Distyl AI的Analytics Insight引擎开发中所经历的那样。它在企业客户中的早期部署突出了三个核心挑战。首先，数据分析师希望支持编写非常复杂的SQL查询。其次，请求是临时的，因此需要低延迟。最后，生成需要理解特定于领域的术语和实践。在大型语言模型的支持下，我们的文本到SQL生成管道的设计和实现解决了这些挑战。我们方法的核心承租人依赖于我们在预处理阶段提取的外部知识，依赖于在查询生成时检索适当的外部知识，以及依赖于按照基于CTE的分层结构分解SQL查询生成。最后，适应框架利用反馈来更新外部知识，进而随着时间的推移改进查询生成。我们将概述我们的端到端方法，并重点介绍在推理过程中生成SQL的操作符。学科：计算与语言(cs.CL)；人工智能(cs.AI)；数据库(cs.DB)；机器学习(cs.LG)引用AS：arxiv：2406.12104cs.CL

[NLP-127] Whos asking? User personas and the mechanics of latent misalignment
[NLP-127] 谁问？用户角色和潜在失调的机制

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12094
作者: Asma Ghandeharioun,Ann Yuan,Marius Guerard,Emily Reif,Michael A. Lepori,Lucas Dixon
关键词: misaligned capabilities remain, capabilities remain latent, investments in improving, misaligned capabilities, capabilities remain
中文关键词: 失调的能力仍然存在，能力仍然潜在，对改善的投资，失调的能力，能力仍然存在
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Despite investments in improving model safety, studies show that misaligned capabilities remain latent in safety-tuned models. In this work, we shed light on the mechanics of this phenomenon. First, we show that even when model generations are safe, harmful content can persist in hidden representations and can be extracted by decoding from earlier layers. Then, we show that whether the model divulges such content depends significantly on its perception of who it is talking to, which we refer to as user persona. In fact, we find manipulating user persona to be even more effective for eliciting harmful content than direct attempts to control model refusal. We study both natural language prompting and activation steering as control methods and show that activation steering is significantly more effective at bypassing safety filters. We investigate why certain personas break model safeguards and find that they enable the model to form more charitable interpretations of otherwise dangerous queries. Finally, we show we can predict a persona’s effect on refusal given only the geometry of its steering vector.
摘要：尽管在改善模型安全性方面进行了投资，但研究表明，在安全调整的模型中，未对齐的能力仍然潜在。在这项工作中，我们阐明了这一现象的机制。首先，我们证明了即使模型生成是安全的，有害内容也可以持续存在于隐藏的表示中，并且可以通过从较早的层解码来提取。然后，我们表明，模型是否泄露此类内容在很大程度上取决于它对与谁交谈的感知，我们将其称为用户角色。事实上，我们发现，与直接尝试控制模型拒绝相比，操纵用户角色甚至更有效地引出有害内容。我们研究了自然语言提示和激活引导作为控制方法，结果表明激活引导在绕过安全过滤器方面明显更有效。我们调查了为什么某些人物角色违反了模型的保障措施，并发现它们使模型能够对其他危险的问题形成更仁慈的解释。最后，我们证明，我们可以预测一个人物角色对拒绝的影响，只需给出其导向向量的几何形状。

[NLP-128] Is poisoning a real threat to LLM alignment? Maybe more so than you think
[NLP-128] 中毒是对LLM联盟的真正威胁吗？也许比你想象的还要多

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12091
作者: Pankayaraj Pathmanathan,Souradip Chakraborty,Xiangyu Liu,Yongyuan Liang,Furong Huang
关键词: Human Feedback, Proximal Policy Optimization, Direct Policy Optimization, Large Language Models, Recent advancements
中文关键词: 人类反馈、近端策略优化、直接策略优化、大型语言模型、最新进展
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Cryptography and Security (cs.CR)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recent advancements in Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF) have significantly impacted the alignment of Large Language Models (LLMs). The sensitivity of reinforcement learning algorithms such as Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) has led to new line work on Direct Policy Optimization (DPO), which treats RLHF in a supervised learning framework. The increased practical use of these RLHF methods warrants an analysis of their vulnerabilities. In this work, we investigate the vulnerabilities of DPO to poisoning attacks under different scenarios and compare the effectiveness of preference poisoning, a first of its kind. We comprehensively analyze DPO’s vulnerabilities under different types of attacks, i.e., backdoor and non-backdoor attacks, and different poisoning methods across a wide array of language models, i.e., LLama 7B, Mistral 7B, and Gemma 7B. We find that unlike PPO-based methods, which, when it comes to backdoor attacks, require at least 4% of the data to be poisoned to elicit harmful behavior, we exploit the true vulnerabilities of DPO more simply so we can poison the model with only as much as 0.5% of the data. We further investigate the potential reasons behind the vulnerability and how well this vulnerability translates into backdoor vs non-backdoor attacks.
摘要：带人反馈的强化学习(RLHF)的最新进展对大型语言模型(LLM)的对齐产生了重大影响。强化学习算法的敏感性，如最近策略优化(PPO)，导致了直接策略优化(DPO)的新工作，它在监督学习框架中处理RLHF。这些RLHF方法的实际使用越来越多，因此有理由对其脆弱性进行分析。在这项工作中，我们调查了DPO在不同场景下对中毒攻击的脆弱性，并比较了偏好中毒的有效性，这是第一次。我们全面分析了DPO在不同类型的攻击下的漏洞，即后门攻击和非后门攻击，以及不同的中毒方法，跨越了广泛的语言模型，即：大羊驼7B、米斯特拉尔7B和杰玛7B。我们发现，与基于PPO的方法不同，当涉及到后门攻击时，需要至少4%的数据被毒化才能引发有害行为，而我们更简单地利用DPO的真正漏洞，因此我们只需使用多达0.5%的数据就可以毒害模型。我们进一步调查了该漏洞背后的潜在原因，以及该漏洞在多大程度上转化为后门攻击与非后门攻击。

[NLP-129] When Reasoning Meets Information Aggregation: A Case Study with Sports Narratives
[NLP-129] 当推理遇到信息聚合：体育叙事的案例研究

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12084
作者: Yebowen Hu,Kaiqiang Song,Sangwoo Cho,Xiaoyang Wang,Wenlin Yao,Hassan Foroosh,Dong Yu,Fei Liu
关键词: LLM accurately aggregates, accurately aggregates relevant, LLM, aggregates relevant information, Reasoning
中文关键词: LLM准确汇总，准确汇总相关，LLM，汇总相关信息，推理
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Reasoning is most powerful when an LLM accurately aggregates relevant information. We examine the critical role of information aggregation in reasoning by requiring the LLM to analyze sports narratives. To succeed at this task, an LLM must infer points from actions, identify related entities, attribute points accurately to players and teams, and compile key statistics to draw conclusions. We conduct comprehensive experiments with real NBA basketball data and present SportsGen, a new method to synthesize game narratives. By synthesizing data, we can rigorously evaluate LLMs’ reasoning capabilities under complex scenarios with varying narrative lengths and density of information. Our findings show that most models, including GPT-4o, often fail to accurately aggregate basketball scores due to frequent scoring patterns. Open-source models like Llama-3 further suffer from significant score hallucinations. Finally, the effectiveness of reasoning is influenced by narrative complexity, information density, and domain-specific terms, highlighting the challenges in analytical reasoning tasks.
摘要：当LLM准确地聚合相关信息时，推理是最强大的。我们通过要求LLM分析体育叙事来检验信息聚集在推理中的关键作用。为了成功完成这项任务，LLM必须从行动中推断点数，识别相关实体，准确地将点数分配给球员和球队，并汇编关键统计数据以得出结论。我们使用真实的NBA篮球数据进行了全面的实验，并提出了一种新的合成比赛叙事的方法SportsGen。通过综合数据，我们可以严格评估LLMS在不同叙事长度和信息密度的复杂场景下的推理能力。我们的发现表明，由于频繁的得分模式，包括GPT-40在内的大多数模型经常无法准确地汇总篮球得分。像Llama-3这样的开源模型还会受到严重的分数幻觉的影响。最后，推理的有效性受到叙事复杂性、信息密度和特定领域术语的影响，这突显了分析推理任务中的挑战。

[NLP-130] COMMUNITY-CROSS-INSTRUCT: Unsupervised Instruction Generation for Aligning Large Language Models to Online Communities
[NLP-130] COMUNITY-CROSS-INQUALCT：用于将大型语言模型与在线社区保持一致的无监督指令生成

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12074
作者: Zihao He,Rebecca Dorn,Siyi Guo,Minh Duc Chu,Kristina Lerman
关键词: Social scientists, prone to biases, scientists use surveys, surveys to probe, probe the opinions
中文关键词: 社会科学家，容易产生偏见，科学家使用调查、调查来探索、探索意见
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Social scientists use surveys to probe the opinions and beliefs of populations, but these methods are slow, costly, and prone to biases. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) enable creating computational representations or “digital twins” of populations that generate human-like responses mimicking the population’s language, styles, and attitudes. We introduce Community-Cross-Instruct, an unsupervised framework for aligning LLMs to online communities to elicit their beliefs. Given a corpus of a community’s online discussions, Community-Cross-Instruct automatically generates instruction-output pairs by an advanced LLM to (1) finetune an foundational LLM to faithfully represent that community, and (2) evaluate the alignment of the finetuned model to the community. We demonstrate the method’s utility in accurately representing political and fitness communities on Reddit. Unlike prior methods requiring human-authored instructions, Community-Cross-Instruct generates instructions in a fully unsupervised manner, enhancing scalability and generalization across domains. This work enables cost-effective and automated surveying of diverse online communities.
摘要：社会科学家使用调查来调查人群的意见和信仰，但这些方法速度慢、成本高，而且容易产生偏见。大型语言模型(LLM)的最新进展使创建群体的计算表示或“数字孪生”成为可能，这些群体产生模仿群体的语言、风格和态度的类似人类的反应。我们引入了社区-交叉指令，这是一个无监督的框架，用于将LLM与在线社区结盟，以引发他们的信念。在给定社区在线讨论语料库的情况下，社区-交叉指令由高级LLM自动生成指令-输出对，以(1)精调基础LLM以忠实地代表该社区，以及(2)评估精调模型与社区的一致性。我们演示了该方法在Reddit上准确表示政治和健身社区的实用性。与以前需要人工编写指令的方法不同，Community-Cross-Indict以完全无监督的方式生成指令，增强了跨域的可伸缩性和通用性。这项工作能够对不同的在线社区进行具有成本效益的自动化调查。

[NLP-131] Satyrn: A Platform for Analytics Augmented Generation
[NLP-131] Satyrn：分析增强一代平台

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12069
作者: Marko Sterbentz,Cameron Barrie,Shubham Shahi,Abhratanu Dutta,Donna Hooshmand,Harper Pack,Kristian J. Hammond
关键词: retrieval augmented generation, capable of producing, powerful method, method for improving, improving accuracy
中文关键词: 检索增强生成，能够产生，强大的方法，改进的方法，提高准确性
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large language models (LLMs) are capable of producing documents, and retrieval augmented generation (RAG) has shown itself to be a powerful method for improving accuracy without sacrificing fluency. However, not all information can be retrieved from text. We propose an approach that uses the analysis of structured data to generate fact sets that are used to guide generation in much the same way that retrieved documents are used in RAG. This analytics augmented generation (AAG) approach supports the ability to utilize standard analytic techniques to generate facts that are then converted to text and passed to an LLM. We present a neurosymbolic platform, Satyrn that leverages AAG to produce accurate, fluent, and coherent reports grounded in large scale databases. In our experiments, we find that Satyrn generates reports in which over 86% accurate claims while maintaining high levels of fluency and coherence, even when using smaller language models such as Mistral-7B, as compared to GPT-4 Code Interpreter in which just 57% of claims are accurate.
摘要：大型语言模型能够生成文档，而检索增强生成(RAG)已被证明是在不牺牲流畅性的情况下提高准确率的一种强有力的方法。然而，并非所有信息都可以从文本中检索到。我们提出了一种方法，使用结构化数据的分析来生成事实集，这些事实集用于指导生成，其方式与检索到的文档在RAG中使用的方式大致相同。这种分析增强生成(AAG)方法支持利用标准分析技术生成事实，然后将其转换为文本并传递给LLM。我们提供了一个神经符号平台，Satyrn，它利用AAG生成基于大型数据库的准确、流畅和连贯的报告。在我们的实验中，我们发现Satyrn生成的报告中有超过86%的声明是准确的，同时保持了高水平的流利性和连贯性，即使在使用较小的语言模型(如Mistral-7B)时也是如此，而在GPT-4代码解释器中，只有57%的声明是准确的。

[NLP-132] Language Models are Surprisingly Fragile to Drug Names in Biomedical Benchmarks
[NLP-132] 语言模型对生物医学基准中的药物名称出奇地脆弱

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12066
作者: Jack Gallifant,Shan Chen,Pedro Moreira,Nikolaj Munch,Mingye Gao,Jackson Pond,Leo Anthony Celi,Hugo Aerts,Thomas Hartvigsen,Danielle Bitterman
关键词: semantically equivalent phrases, natural language expressions, requires consistent reasoning, knowledge is context-dependent, context-dependent and requires
中文关键词: 语义等效短语、自然语言表达，需要一致的推理，知识依赖于上下文，依赖于上下文，需要
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: submitted for review, total 15 pages

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Medical knowledge is context-dependent and requires consistent reasoning across various natural language expressions of semantically equivalent phrases. This is particularly crucial for drug names, where patients often use brand names like Advil or Tylenol instead of their generic equivalents. To study this, we create a new robustness dataset, RABBITS, to evaluate performance differences on medical benchmarks after swapping brand and generic drug names using physician expert annotations. We assess both open-source and API-based LLMs on MedQA and MedMCQA, revealing a consistent performance drop ranging from 1-10%. Furthermore, we identify a potential source of this fragility as the contamination of test data in widely used pre-training datasets. All code is accessible at this https URL, and a HuggingFace leaderboard is available at this https URL. Comments: submitted for review, total 15 pages Subjects: Computation and Language (cs.CL) Cite as: arXiv:2406.12066 [cs.CL] (or arXiv:2406.12066v2 [cs.CL] for this version) https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2406.12066 Focus to learn more arXiv-issued DOI via DataCite
摘要：医学知识依赖于语境，需要在语义等价短语的各种自然语言表达上进行一致的推理。这对药物名称尤其关键，因为患者经常使用Advil或Tylenol等品牌名称，而不是它们的仿制药。为了研究这一点，我们创建了一个新的健壮性数据集，兔子，以评估使用医生专家注释交换品牌和仿制药名称后在医疗基准上的性能差异。我们在MedQA和MedMCQA上对开源和基于API的LLM进行了评估，发现性能持续下降，降幅在1-10%之间。此外，我们确定了这种脆弱性的一个潜在来源，即广泛使用的预训练数据集中的测试数据受到污染。所有代码都可以通过此HTTPS URL访问，HuggingFace排行榜也可通过此HTTPS URL访问。评论：提交审查，共15页主题：计算与语言(cs.CL)引用为：arxiv：2406.12066cs.CLhttps://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2406.12066 Focus通过DataCite了解更多arxiv发布的文档

[NLP-133] Not Eliminate but Aggregate: Post-Hoc Control over Mixture-of-Experts to Address Shortcut Shifts in Natural Language Understanding
[NLP-133] 不是消除而是聚合：对混合专家的事后控制以解决自然语言理解中的时间变化

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12060
作者: Ukyo Honda,Tatsushi Oka,Peinan Zhang,Masato Mita
关键词: natural language understanding, exploit simple patterns, Recent models, patterns in datasets, natural language
中文关键词: 自然语言理解、利用简单模式、最新模型、数据集中的模式、自然语言
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: Accepted to TACL (pre-MIT Press publication version, 21 pages, 5 figures)

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recent models for natural language understanding are inclined to exploit simple patterns in datasets, commonly known as shortcuts. These shortcuts hinge on spurious correlations between labels and latent features existing in the training data. At inference time, shortcut-dependent models are likely to generate erroneous predictions under distribution shifts, particularly when some latent features are no longer correlated with the labels. To avoid this, previous studies have trained models to eliminate the reliance on shortcuts. In this study, we explore a different direction: pessimistically aggregating the predictions of a mixture-of-experts, assuming each expert captures relatively different latent features. The experimental results demonstrate that our post-hoc control over the experts significantly enhances the model’s robustness to the distribution shift in shortcuts. Besides, we show that our approach has some practical advantages. We also analyze our model and provide results to support the assumption.
摘要：最近的自然语言理解模型倾向于利用数据集中的简单模式，也就是通常所说的快捷方式。这些捷径依赖于训练数据中存在的标签和潜在特征之间的虚假相关性。在推理时，依赖于捷径的模型很可能在分布变化下产生错误的预测，特别是当一些潜在特征不再与标签相关时。为了避免这种情况，以前的研究已经训练了模型，以消除对捷径的依赖。在这项研究中，我们探索了一个不同的方向：悲观地聚合混合专家的预测，假设每个专家捕捉到相对不同的潜在特征。实验结果表明，我们对专家的事后控制显著增强了模型对捷径分布变化的鲁棒性。此外，我们还证明了我们的方法具有一定的实用优势。我们还分析了我们的模型，并提供了支持这一假设的结果。

[NLP-134] WellDunn: On the Robustness and Explainability of Language Models and Large Language Models in Identifying Wellness Dimensions
[NLP-134] WellDunn：语言模型和大型语言模型在识别健康维度方面的稳健性和可解释性

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12058
作者: Seyedali Mohammadi,Edward Raff,Jinendra Malekar,Vedant Palit,Francis Ferraro,Manas Gaur
关键词: sufficient litmus test, Language Models, model utility, heightened risk, risk of adverse
中文关键词: 充分的试金石测试、语言模型、模型效用、风险升高、不良风险
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 26 pages, including reference and appendix sections, 8 figures, and 16 tables

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Language Models (LMs) are being proposed for mental health applications where the heightened risk of adverse outcomes means predictive performance may not be a sufficient litmus test of a model’s utility in clinical practice. A model that can be trusted for practice should have a correspondence between explanation and clinical determination, yet no prior research has examined the attention fidelity of these models and their effect on ground truth explanations. We introduce an evaluation design that focuses on the robustness and explainability of LMs in identifying Wellness Dimensions (WD). We focus on two mental health and well-being datasets: (a) Multi-label Classification-based MultiWD, and (b) WellXplain for evaluating attention mechanism veracity against expert-labeled explanations. The labels are based on Halbert Dunn’s theory of wellness, which gives grounding to our evaluation. We reveal four surprising results about LMs/LLMs: (1) Despite their human-like capabilities, GPT-3.5/4 lag behind RoBERTa, and MedAlpaca, a fine-tuned LLM fails to deliver any remarkable improvements in performance or explanations. (2) Re-examining LMs’ predictions based on a confidence-oriented loss function reveals a significant performance drop. (3) Across all LMs/LLMs, the alignment between attention and explanations remains low, with LLMs scoring a dismal 0.0. (4) Most mental health-specific LMs/LLMs overlook domain-specific knowledge and undervalue explanations, causing these discrepancies. This study highlights the need for further research into their consistency and explanations in mental health and well-being.
摘要：语言模型(LMS)被提出用于心理健康应用，在这些应用中，不良结果的高风险意味着预测能力可能不是模型在临床实践中实用的充分试金石。一个可以信赖用于实践的模型应该在解释和临床确定之间具有一致性，然而之前没有研究检验这些模型的注意保真度及其对基本事实解释的影响。我们介绍了一种评估设计，其重点是LMS在识别健康维度(WD)时的稳健性和可解释性。我们集中在两个心理健康和幸福感数据集：(A)基于多标签分类的MultiWD，和(B)WellXPlain，用于评估注意机制的准确性与专家标签的解释。这些标签是基于哈尔伯特·邓恩的健康理论，这为我们的评估奠定了基础。我们揭示了关于LMS/LLMS的四个令人惊讶的结果：(1)尽管它们具有与人类相似的能力，GPT-3.5/4落后于Roberta和MedAlpaca，但经过微调的LLM在性能或解释方面并没有带来任何显著的改进。(2)基于以置信度为导向的损失函数重新检验LMS的预测，发现性能显著下降。(3)在所有LMS/LLMS中，注意和解释之间的一致性仍然很低，LLMS得分为令人沮丧的0.0。(4)大多数针对心理健康的LMS/LLM忽视了特定领域的知识，低估了解释，导致了这些差异。这项研究强调了进一步研究它们在心理健康和幸福感方面的一致性和解释的必要性。

[NLP-135] InternalInspector I2: Robust Confidence Estimation in LLMs through Internal States
[NLP-135] InternalInspector I2：通过内部状态对LLM进行稳健信心估计

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12053
作者: Mohammad Beigi,Ying Shen,Runing Yang,Zihao Lin,Qifan Wang,Ankith Mohan,Jianfeng He,Ming Jin,Chang-Tien Lu,Lifu Huang
关键词: Large Language Models, generating reliable outputs, frequently producing high-confidence, producing high-confidence inaccuracies, Large Language
中文关键词: 大型语言模型，生成可靠的输出，经常产生高置信度，产生高置信度的不准确度，大型语言
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 8 pages

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Despite their vast capabilities, Large Language Models (LLMs) often struggle with generating reliable outputs, frequently producing high-confidence inaccuracies known as hallucinations. Addressing this challenge, our research introduces InternalInspector, a novel framework designed to enhance confidence estimation in LLMs by leveraging contrastive learning on internal states including attention states, feed-forward states, and activation states of all layers. Unlike existing methods that primarily focus on the final activation state, InternalInspector conducts a comprehensive analysis across all internal states of every layer to accurately identify both correct and incorrect prediction processes. By benchmarking InternalInspector against existing confidence estimation methods across various natural language understanding and generation tasks, including factual question answering, commonsense reasoning, and reading comprehension, InternalInspector achieves significantly higher accuracy in aligning the estimated confidence scores with the correctness of the LLM’s predictions and lower calibration error. Furthermore, InternalInspector excels at HaluEval, a hallucination detection benchmark, outperforming other internal-based confidence estimation methods in this task.
摘要：尽管大型语言模型(LLM)具有巨大的能力，但它们经常难以生成可靠的输出，经常会产生被称为幻觉的高置信度不准确。为了应对这一挑战，我们的研究引入了InternalInspector，这是一个新的框架，旨在通过利用对所有层的内部状态(包括注意状态、前馈状态和激活状态)的对比学习来增强LLMS中的置信度估计。与主要关注最终激活状态的现有方法不同，InternalInspector对每一层的所有内部状态进行全面分析，以准确识别正确和错误的预测过程。通过将InternalInspector与各种自然语言理解和生成任务(包括事实问题回答、常识推理和阅读理解)的现有置信度估计方法进行基准比较，InternalInspector在将估计的置信度分数与LLM预测的正确性进行对齐方面实现了显著更高的准确性，并降低了校准误差。此外，InternalInspector在幻觉检测基准HaluEval方面表现出色，在这项任务中表现优于其他基于内部的置信度估计方法。

[NLP-136] UniGLM: Training One Unified Language Model for Text-Attributed Graphs
[NLP-136] UniGLM：为文本属性图训练一种统一语言模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12052
作者: Yi Fang,Dongzhe Fan,Sirui Ding,Ninghao Liu,Qiaoyu Tan
关键词: relational knowledge systems, knowledge systems, recommendation systems, Representation learning, relational knowledge
中文关键词: 关系知识系统、知识系统、推荐系统、表示学习、关系知识
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Information Retrieval (cs.IR); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Representation learning on text-attributed graphs (TAGs), where nodes are represented by textual descriptions, is crucial for textual and relational knowledge systems and recommendation systems. Currently, state-of-the-art embedding methods for TAGs primarily focus on fine-tuning language models (e.g., BERT) using structure-aware training signals. While effective, these methods are tailored for individual TAG and cannot generalize across various graph scenarios. Given the shared textual space, leveraging multiple TAGs for joint fine-tuning, aligning text and graph structure from different aspects, would be more beneficial. Motivated by this, we introduce a novel Unified Graph Language Model (UniGLM) framework, the first graph embedding model that generalizes well to both in-domain and cross-domain TAGs. Specifically, UniGLM is trained over multiple TAGs with different domains and scales using self-supervised contrastive learning. UniGLM includes an adaptive positive sample selection technique for identifying structurally similar nodes and a lazy contrastive module that is devised to accelerate training by minimizing repetitive encoding calculations. Extensive empirical results across 9 benchmark TAGs demonstrate UniGLM’s efficacy against leading embedding baselines in terms of generalization (various downstream tasks and backbones) and transfer learning (in and out of domain scenarios). The code is available at this https URL.
摘要：文本属性图(标签)上的表示学习对于文本和关系知识系统和推荐系统来说是至关重要的。目前，最先进的标签嵌入方法主要集中在使用结构感知训练信号来微调语言模型(例如，BERT)。虽然这些方法很有效，但它们是为单个标记量身定做的，不能跨各种图形场景进行泛化。在共享文本空间的情况下，利用多个标记进行联合微调，从不同方面对齐文本和图表结构将更有利。受此启发，我们提出了一种新的统一图语言模型(UniGLM)框架，这是第一个对域内和跨域标签都能很好地泛化的图嵌入模型。具体地说，UniGLM使用自监督对比学习对不同域和尺度的多个标签进行训练。UniGLM包括用于识别结构相似节点的自适应正样本选择技术和懒惰对比模块，该模块旨在通过最小化重复编码计算来加速训练。在9个基准标签上的广泛的经验结果证明了UniGLM在泛化(各种下游任务和主干)和迁移学习(域内和域外场景)方面相对于领先的嵌入基线的有效性。代码可在此HTTPS URL上找到。

[NLP-137] Learn Beyond The Answer: Training Language Models with Reflection for Mathematical Reasoning
[NLP-137] 超越答案的学习：通过数学推理的反思训练语言模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12050
作者: Zhihan Zhang,Zhenwen Liang,Wenhao Yu,Dian Yu,Mengzhao Jia,Dong Yu,Meng Jiang
关键词: Supervised fine-tuning enhances, Supervised fine-tuning, mathematical reasoning tasks, fine-tuning enhances, enhances the problem-solving
中文关键词: 监督微调增强，监督微调，数学推理任务，微调增强，增强问题解决能力
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Supervised fine-tuning enhances the problem-solving abilities of language models across various mathematical reasoning tasks. To maximize such benefits, existing research focuses on broadening the training set with various data augmentation techniques, which is effective for standard single-round question-answering settings. Our work introduces a novel technique aimed at cultivating a deeper understanding of the training problems at hand, enhancing performance not only in standard settings but also in more complex scenarios that require reflective thinking. Specifically, we propose reflective augmentation, a method that embeds problem reflection into each training instance. It trains the model to consider alternative perspectives and engage with abstractions and analogies, thereby fostering a thorough comprehension through reflective reasoning. Extensive experiments validate the achievement of our aim, underscoring the unique advantages of our method and its complementary nature relative to existing augmentation techniques.
摘要：有监督的微调提高了语言模型在各种数学推理任务中的问题解决能力。为了使这些好处最大化，现有的研究重点是用各种数据扩充技术来扩大训练集，这对标准的单轮问答设置是有效的。我们的工作引入了一种新的技术，旨在培养对手头培训问题的更深层次的理解，不仅在标准环境下提高表现，而且在需要反思思维的更复杂的情况下也提高表现。具体地说，我们提出了反射增强，一种将问题反映嵌入到每个训练实例中的方法。它训练模型考虑不同的观点，并进行抽象和类比，从而通过反思性推理培养全面的理解。广泛的实验验证了我们目标的实现，强调了我们方法的独特优势及其相对于现有增强技术的互补性。

[NLP-138] tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains
[NLP-138] tau-table：现实世界领域中工具-代理-用户交互的基准

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12045
作者: Shunyu Yao,Noah Shinn,Pedram Razavi,Karthik Narasimhan
关键词: real world applications, Existing benchmarks, test language agents, world applications, domain-specific API tools
中文关键词: 现实世界应用程序、现有基准测试、测试语言代理、世界应用程序、特定领域的API工具
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Existing benchmarks do not test language agents on their interaction with human users or ability to follow domain-specific rules, both of which are vital for deploying them in real world applications. We propose \tau -bench, a benchmark emulating dynamic conversations between a user (simulated by language models) and a language agent provided with domain-specific API tools and policy guidelines. We employ an efficient and faithful evaluation process that compares the database state at the end of a conversation with the annotated goal state. We also propose a new metric (pass^k) to evaluate the reliability of agent behavior over multiple trials. Our experiments show that even state-of-the-art function calling agents (like gpt-4o) succeed on 50% of the tasks, and are quite inconsistent (pass^8 25% in retail). Our findings point to the need for methods that can improve the ability of agents to act consistently and follow rules reliably.
摘要：现有的基准测试不会测试语言代理与人类用户的交互或遵循特定领域规则的能力，而这两者对于在现实世界应用程序中部署它们至关重要。我们提出了\tau -bridge，这是一个模拟用户（由语言模型模拟）和语言代理之间动态对话的基准，提供了特定于领域的API工具和策略指南。我们采用一个高效且忠实的评估过程，将对话结束时的数据库状态与带注释的目标状态进行比较。我们还提出了一个新的指标（通过’ k）来评估多次试验中代理行为的可靠性。我们的实验表明，即使是最先进的功能调用代理（如gpt-4 o）也能成功完成50%的任务，并且非常不一致（在零售中通过’ 8 25%）。我们的研究结果指出，需要能够提高代理人一致行动并可靠遵守规则的能力的方法。

[NLP-139] Soft Prompting for Unlearning in Large Language Models
[NLP-139] 大型语言模型中取消学习的软预算

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12038
作者: Karuna Bhaila,Minh-Hao Van,Xintao Wu
关键词: Large Language Models, perform in-context learning, Language Models, Large Language, pre-trained models
中文关键词: 大型语言模型，执行上下文学习，语言模型，大型语言，预训练模型
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The widespread popularity of Large Language Models (LLMs), partly due to their unique ability to perform in-context learning, has also brought to light the importance of ethical and safety considerations when deploying these pre-trained models. In this work, we focus on investigating machine unlearning for LLMs motivated by data protection regulations. In contrast to the growing literature on fine-tuning methods to achieve unlearning, we focus on a comparatively lightweight alternative called soft prompting to realize the unlearning of a subset of training data. With losses designed to enforce forgetting as well as utility preservation, our framework \textbfSoft \textbfPrompting for \textbfUn\textbflearning (SPUL) learns prompt tokens that can be appended to an arbitrary query to induce unlearning of specific examples at inference time without updating LLM parameters. We conduct a rigorous evaluation of the proposed method and our results indicate that SPUL can significantly improve the trade-off between utility and forgetting in the context of text classification with LLMs. We further validate our method using multiple LLMs to highlight the scalability of our framework and provide detailed insights into the choice of hyperparameters and the influence of the size of unlearning data. Our implementation is available at \urlthis https URL.
摘要：大型语言模型(LLM)的广泛流行，部分是由于它们执行情景学习的独特能力，也揭示了在部署这些预先训练的模型时伦理和安全考虑的重要性。在这项工作中，我们重点研究了受数据保护规则驱动的LLM的机器遗忘问题。与越来越多的关于微调方法来实现遗忘的文献相反，我们专注于一种相对轻量级的替代方案，称为软提示，以实现对训练数据子集的遗忘。由于损失被设计为强制遗忘和实用程序保存，我们的框架\textbfSoft\extbfPromting for\textbfUn\extbfLearning(SPUL)学习提示令牌，这些令牌可以附加到任意查询，以在推理时导致对特定示例的忘记，而不需要更新LLM参数。我们对所提出的方法进行了严格的评估，结果表明，在使用LLMS进行文本分类的情况下，SPUL能够显著改善实用性和健忘性之间的权衡。我们使用多个LLM进一步验证了我们的方法，以突出我们框架的可扩展性，并提供了关于超参数的选择和遗忘数据大小的影响的详细见解。我们的实施可在此HTTPS URL\url获得。

[NLP-140] MedCalc-Bench: Evaluating Large Language Models for Medical Calculations
[NLP-140] MedCalc-Bench：评估用于医学计算的大型语言模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12036
作者: Nikhil Khandekar,Qiao Jin,Guangzhi Xiong,Soren Dunn,Serina S Applebaum,Zain Anwar,Maame Sarfo-Gyamfi,Conrad W Safranek,Abid A Anwar,Andrew Zhang,Aidan Gilson,Maxwell B Singer,Amisha Dave,Andrew Taylor,Aidong Zhang,Qingyu Chen,Zhiyong Lu
关键词: large language models, question-answering involving domain, evaluating large language, involving domain knowledge, language models
中文关键词: 大型语言模型、涉及领域的问答、评估大型语言、涉及领域知识、语言模型
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: Github link: this https URL HuggingFace link: this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:As opposed to evaluating computation and logic-based reasoning, current bench2 marks for evaluating large language models (LLMs) in medicine are primarily focused on question-answering involving domain knowledge and descriptive rea4 soning. While such qualitative capabilities are vital to medical diagnosis, in real5 world scenarios, doctors frequently use clinical calculators that follow quantitative equations and rule-based reasoning paradigms for evidence-based decision support. To this end, we propose MedCalc-Bench, a first-of-its-kind dataset focused on evaluating the medical calculation capability of LLMs. MedCalc-Bench contains an evaluation set of over 1000 manually reviewed instances from 55 different medical calculation tasks. Each instance in MedCalc-Bench consists of a patient note, a question requesting to compute a specific medical value, a ground truth answer, and a step-by-step explanation showing how the answer is obtained. While our evaluation results show the potential of LLMs in this area, none of them are effective enough for clinical settings. Common issues include extracting the incorrect entities, not using the correct equation or rules for a calculation task, or incorrectly performing the arithmetic for the computation. We hope our study highlights the quantitative knowledge and reasoning gaps in LLMs within medical settings, encouraging future improvements of LLMs for various clinical calculation tasks.
摘要：与评估计算和基于逻辑的推理不同，目前用于评估医学大语言模型的基准2主要集中在涉及领域知识的问答和描述性推理。虽然这样的定性能力对医疗诊断至关重要，但在现实世界中，医生经常使用遵循定量方程和基于规则的推理范例的临床计算器来提供基于证据的决策支持。为此，我们提出了MedCalc-BENCH，这是首个专注于评估LLMS医疗计算能力的数据集。MedCalc-BENCH包含来自55个不同医疗计算任务的1000多个手动审查实例的评估集。MedCalc-BENCH中的每个实例都包含一个患者笔记、一个请求计算特定医疗价值的问题、一个基本事实答案，以及一个显示如何获得答案的逐步说明。虽然我们的评估结果显示了LLMS在这一领域的潜力，但没有一种方法对临床设置足够有效。常见问题包括提取不正确的实体、没有为计算任务使用正确的公式或规则，或者不正确地执行计算的算法。我们希望我们的研究突出了医学环境中LLM中的定量知识和推理差距，鼓励LLM未来在各种临床计算任务中的改进。

[NLP-141] Self-MoE: Towards Compositional Large Language Models with Self-Specialized Experts
[NLP-141] 自我教育部：与自我专业化的专家一起迈向合成大型语言模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12034
作者: Junmo Kang,Leonid Karlinsky,Hongyin Luo,Zhen Wang,Jacob Hansen,James Glass,David Cox,Rameswar Panda,Rogerio Feris,Alan Ritter
关键词: self-specialized experts, MiXture of Self-specialized, named MiXSE, transforms a monolithic, self-specialized
中文关键词: 自我专业化专家，Self-specialized的MiXture，名为MiXSE，转变了一个单一的、自我专业化的
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We present Self-MoE, an approach that transforms a monolithic LLM into a compositional, modular system of self-specialized experts, named MiXSE (MiXture of Self-specialized Experts). Our approach leverages self-specialization, which constructs expert modules using self-generated synthetic data, each equipped with a shared base LLM and incorporating self-optimized routing. This allows for dynamic and capability-specific handling of various target tasks, enhancing overall capabilities, without extensive human-labeled data and added parameters. Our empirical results reveal that specializing LLMs may exhibit potential trade-offs in performances on non-specialized tasks. On the other hand, our Self-MoE demonstrates substantial improvements over the base LLM across diverse benchmarks such as knowledge, reasoning, math, and coding. It also consistently outperforms other methods, including instance merging and weight merging, while offering better flexibility and interpretability by design with semantic experts and routing. Our findings highlight the critical role of modularity and the potential of self-improvement in achieving efficient, scalable, and adaptable systems.
摘要：我们提出了一种方法–Self-MOE，它将一个单一的LLM转换成一个组合的、模块化的自专业专家系统，称为MiXSE(混合自专业专家)。我们的方法利用自我专门化，它使用自己生成的合成数据来构建专家模块，每个模块都配备了一个共享的基本LLM，并结合了自我优化的路由。这允许对各种目标任务进行动态和特定于功能的处理，从而增强整体功能，而无需大量人工标记的数据和添加的参数。我们的实证结果表明，专门化的LLM可能在非专门化任务的绩效上表现出潜在的权衡。另一方面，我们的Self-MoE在知识、推理、数学和编码等不同的基准测试中显示出比基本LLM有实质性的改进。它的性能也一直优于其他方法，包括实例合并和权重合并，同时通过与语义专家和路由的设计提供更好的灵活性和可解释性。我们的发现突出了模块化的关键作用和自我改进的潜力，以实现高效、可扩展和可适应的系统。

[NLP-142] Unveiling and Mitigating Bias in Mental Health Analysis with Large Language Models
[NLP-142] 利用大型语言模型揭示和缓解心理健康分析中的偏见

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12033
作者: Yuqing Wang,Yun Zhao,Sara Alessandra Keller,Anne de Hond,Marieke M. van Buchem,Malvika Pillai,Tina Hernandez-Boussard
关键词: demonstrated strong capabilities, large language models, advancement of large, large language, demonstrated strong
中文关键词: 展示了强大的能力，大型语言模型，大型语言的进步，展示了强大的
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: In submission; Data and code are available at: this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The advancement of large language models (LLMs) has demonstrated strong capabilities across various applications, including mental health analysis. However, existing studies have focused on predictive performance, leaving the critical issue of fairness underexplored, posing significant risks to vulnerable populations. Despite acknowledging potential biases, previous works have lacked thorough investigations into these biases and their impacts. To address this gap, we systematically evaluate biases across seven social factors (e.g., gender, age, religion) using ten LLMs with different prompting methods on eight diverse mental health datasets. Our results show that GPT-4 achieves the best overall balance in performance and fairness among LLMs, although it still lags behind domain-specific models like MentalRoBERTa in some cases. Additionally, our tailored fairness-aware prompts can effectively mitigate bias in mental health predictions, highlighting the great potential for fair analysis in this field.
摘要：大型语言模型的发展已经在包括心理健康分析在内的各种应用中显示出强大的能力。然而，现有的研究侧重于预测绩效，而对公平这一关键问题的探索不足，给弱势群体带来了重大风险。尽管承认了潜在的偏见，但以前的作品缺乏对这些偏见及其影响的彻底调查。为了解决这一差距，我们系统地评估了七个社会因素(例如，性别、年龄、宗教)的偏见，使用了八个不同的心理健康数据集上的十个具有不同提示方法的LLM。我们的结果表明，GPT-4在性能和公平性方面达到了LLM之间的最佳平衡，但在某些情况下仍然落后于MentalRoBERTa等特定领域的模型。此外，我们量身定做的公平意识提示可以有效地减轻心理健康预测中的偏差，突显出该领域公平分析的巨大潜力。

[NLP-143] Large Scale Transfer Learning for Tabular Data via Language Modeling
[NLP-143] 通过语言建模的表格数据大规模迁移学习

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12031
作者: Josh Gardner,Juan C. Perdomo,Ludwig Schmidt
关键词: Tabular, tabular prediction, Tabular data, data, tabular domain
中文关键词: 表格、表格预测、表格数据、数据、表格域
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Tabular data – structured, heterogeneous, spreadsheet-style data with rows and columns – is widely used in practice across many domains. However, while recent foundation models have reduced the need for developing task-specific datasets and predictors in domains such as language modeling and computer vision, this transfer learning paradigm has not had similar impact in the tabular domain. In this work, we seek to narrow this gap and present TabuLa-8B, a language model for tabular prediction. We define a process for extracting a large, high-quality training dataset from the TabLib corpus, proposing methods for tabular data filtering and quality control. Using the resulting dataset, which comprises over 1.6B rows from 3.1M unique tables, we fine-tune a Llama 3-8B large language model (LLM) for tabular data prediction (classification and binned regression) using a novel packing and attention scheme for tabular prediction. Through evaluation across a test suite of 329 datasets, we find that TabuLa-8B has zero-shot accuracy on unseen tables that is over 15 percentage points (pp) higher than random guessing, a feat that is not possible with existing state-of-the-art tabular prediction models (e.g. XGBoost, TabPFN). In the few-shot setting (1-32 shots), without any fine-tuning on the target datasets, TabuLa-8B is 5-15 pp more accurate than XGBoost and TabPFN models that are explicitly trained on equal, or even up to 16x more data. We release our model, code, and data along with the publication of this paper.
摘要：表格数据–结构化的、异类的、具有行和列的电子表格样式的数据–在许多领域的实践中被广泛使用。然而，虽然最近的基础模型减少了在语言建模和计算机视觉等领域开发特定于任务的数据集和预测器的需要，但这种迁移学习范式并没有在表格领域产生类似的影响。在这项工作中，我们试图缩小这一差距，并提出了一个用于表格预测的语言模型TABULA-8B。我们定义了从TabLib语料库中提取大量高质量训练数据集的过程，提出了表格数据过滤和质量控制的方法。使用生成的数据集(包括来自310万个唯一表的超过1.6亿行)，我们使用一种新的表格预测打包和注意方案对用于表格数据预测(分类和入库回归)的Llama 3-8B大型语言模型(LLM)进行了微调。通过对329个数据集的测试，我们发现TABULA-8B在不可见表格上的零射准确率比随机猜测高出15个百分点(Pp)，这是现有最先进的表格预测模型(如XGBoost，TabPFN)所不可能实现的。在少镜头设置(1-32次)中，在不对目标数据集进行任何微调的情况下，TABULA-8B的精度比XGBoost和TabPFN模型高5-15pp，后者在相同甚至高达16倍的数据上进行了明确的训练。随着这篇论文的发表，我们发布了我们的模型、代码和数据。

[NLP-144] SPA-VL: A Comprehensive Safety Preference Alignment Dataset for Vision Language Model
[NLP-144] SPA-DL：视觉语言模型的全面安全偏好对齐数据集

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12030
作者: Yongting Zhang,Lu Chen,Guodong Zheng,Yifeng Gao,Rui Zheng,Jinlan Fu,Zhenfei Yin,Senjie Jin,Yu Qiao,Xuanjing Huang,Feng Zhao,Tao Gui,Jing Shao
关键词: Vision Language Models, understanding multimodal information, brought unprecedented advances, Vision Language, Language Models
中文关键词: 视觉语言模型，理解多模式信息，带来了前所未有的进步，视觉语言，语言模型
类目: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The emergence of Vision Language Models (VLMs) has brought unprecedented advances in understanding multimodal information. The combination of textual and visual semantics in VLMs is highly complex and diverse, making the safety alignment of these models challenging. Furthermore, due to the limited study on the safety alignment of VLMs, there is a lack of large-scale, high-quality datasets. To address these limitations, we propose a Safety Preference Alignment dataset for Vision Language Models named SPA-VL. In terms of breadth, SPA-VL covers 6 harmfulness domains, 13 categories, and 53 subcategories, and contains 100,788 samples of the quadruple (question, image, chosen response, rejected response). In terms of depth, the responses are collected from 12 open- (e.g., QwenVL) and closed-source (e.g., Gemini) VLMs to ensure diversity. The experimental results indicate that models trained with alignment techniques on the SPA-VL dataset exhibit substantial improvements in harmlessness and helpfulness while maintaining core capabilities. SPA-VL, as a large-scale, high-quality, and diverse dataset, represents a significant milestone in ensuring that VLMs achieve both harmlessness and helpfulness. We have made our code this https URL and SPA-VL dataset url this https URL publicly available.
摘要：视觉语言模型的出现给多通道信息的理解带来了前所未有的进步。VLMS中文本和视觉语义的结合是高度复杂和多样化的，这使得这些模型的安全对齐具有挑战性。此外，由于对超大型机械安全配准的研究有限，缺乏大规模、高质量的数据集。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了一个用于视觉语言模型的安全偏好对齐数据集SPA-VL。在广度上，SPA-VL涵盖6个危害领域，13个类别，53个子类别，包含100788个样本的四重(问题，图像，选择反应，拒绝反应)。在深度方面，从12个开放(如QwenVL)和闭源(如Gemini)VLM收集答复，以确保多样性。实验结果表明，在SPA-VL数据集上使用对齐技术训练的模型在保持核心能力的同时，在无害性和有用性方面显示出显著的改善。SPA-VL作为一个大规模、高质量和多样化的数据集，在确保VLM实现无害和有用方面具有重要的里程碑意义。我们已经使我们的代码此HTTPS URL和SPA-VL数据集URL此HTTPS URL公开可用。

[NLP-145] LiLiuM: eBays Large Language Models for e-commerce
[NLP-145] LiLiuM：eBays电子商务大型语言模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12023
作者: Christian Herold,Michael Kozielski,Leonid Ekimov,Pavel Petrushkov,Pierre-Yves Vandenbussche,Shahram Khadivi
关键词: parameter models developed, in-house to fit, fit eBay specific, series of large, fit eBay
中文关键词: 开发的参数模型，内部适合，适合eBay特定，系列大型，适合eBay
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We introduce the LiLiuM series of large language models (LLMs): 1B, 7B, and 13B parameter models developed 100% in-house to fit eBay’s specific needs in the e-commerce domain. This gives eBay full control over all aspects of the models including license, data, vocabulary, and architecture. We expect these models to be used as a foundation for fine-tuning and instruction-tuning, eliminating dependencies to external models. The LiLiuM LLMs have been trained on 3 trillion tokens of multilingual text from general and e-commerce domain. They perform similar to the popular LLaMA-2 models on English natural language understanding (NLU) benchmarks. At the same time, we outperform LLaMA-2 on non-English NLU tasks, machine translation and on e-commerce specific downstream tasks. As part of our data mixture, we utilize the newly released RedPajama-V2 dataset for training and share our insights regarding data filtering and deduplication. We also discuss in detail how to serialize structured data for use in autoregressive language modeling. We provide insights on the effects of including code and parallel machine translation data in pre-training. Furthermore, we develop our own tokenizer and model vocabulary, customized towards e-commerce. This way, we can achieve up to 34% speed-up in text generation on eBay-specific downstream tasks compared to LLaMA-2. Finally, in relation to LLM pretraining, we show that checkpoint averaging can further improve over the best individual model checkpoint. Subjects: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG) Cite as: arXiv:2406.12023 [cs.CL] (or arXiv:2406.12023v1 [cs.CL] for this version) Submission history From: Christian Herold [view email] [v1] Mon, 17 Jun 2024 18:45:41 UTC (975 KB)
摘要：我们介绍Lilium系列大型语言模型(LLM)：1B、7B和13B参数模型是100%内部开发的，以满足eBay在电子商务领域的特定需求。这让eBay完全控制了模型的所有方面，包括许可证、数据、词汇和体系结构。我们预计这些模型将用作微调和指令调优的基础，消除对外部模型的依赖。Lilium LLMS已经接受了来自一般和电子商务领域的3万亿个多语种文本标记的培训。它们在英语自然语言理解(NLU)基准上的表现类似于流行的骆驼-2模型。与此同时，我们在非英语NLU任务、机器翻译和电子商务特定下游任务上的表现优于Llama-2。作为我们数据混合的一部分，我们利用新发布的RedPajama-V2数据集进行培训，并分享我们对数据过滤和重复数据删除的见解。我们还详细讨论了如何序列化结构化数据以用于自回归语言建模。我们提供了关于在预培训中包括代码和并行机器翻译数据的影响的见解。此外，我们还开发了自己的标记器和模型词汇表，为电子商务定制。这样，与骆驼2号相比，我们可以在eBay特定的下游任务上实现高达34%的文本生成速度。最后，对于LLM预训练，我们证明了检查点平均可以进一步提高最佳个体模型检查点的性能。主题：计算与语言(cs.CL)；机器学习(cs.LG)引用AS：arxiv：2406.12023cs.CL提交历史来自：Christian Herold[查看电子邮件][v1]Mon，17 Jun 202418：45：41 UTC(975KB)

[NLP-146] CItruS: Chunked Instruction-aware State Eviction for Long Sequence Modeling
[NLP-146] CITRUS：用于长序列建模的分块指令感知状态驱逐

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12018
作者: Yu Bai,Xiyuan Zou,Heyan Huang,Sanxing Chen,Marc-Antoine Rondeau,Yang Gao,Jackie Chi Kit Cheung
关键词: gained broad interest, continue to advance, gained broad, broad interest, Long sequence
中文关键词: 获得了广泛的兴趣，继续前进，获得了广泛的兴趣，长序列
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Work in progress

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Long sequence modeling has gained broad interest as large language models (LLMs) continue to advance. Recent research has identified that a large portion of hidden states within the key-value caches of Transformer models can be discarded (also termed evicted) without affecting the perplexity performance in generating long sequences. However, we show that these methods, despite preserving perplexity performance, often drop information that is important for solving downstream tasks, a problem which we call information neglect. To address this issue, we introduce Chunked Instruction-aware State Eviction (CItruS), a novel modeling technique that integrates the attention preferences useful for a downstream task into the eviction process of hidden states. In addition, we design a method for chunked sequence processing to further improve efficiency. Our training-free method exhibits superior performance on long sequence comprehension and retrieval tasks over several strong baselines under the same memory budget, while preserving language modeling perplexity.
摘要：随着大型语言模型的不断发展，长序列建模得到了广泛的关注。最近的研究发现，Transformer模型的键值缓存中的很大一部分隐藏状态可以被丢弃(也称为驱逐)，而不会影响生成长序列的困惑性能。然而，我们发现，尽管这些方法保留了困惑性能，但往往会丢弃对解决下游任务至关重要的信息，这一问题我们称为信息忽略。为了解决这一问题，我们引入了组块指令感知状态驱逐(Citrus)，这是一种新的建模技术，它将对下游任务有用的注意偏好整合到隐藏状态的驱逐过程中。此外，我们还设计了一种分块序列处理方法，进一步提高了效率。在相同的存储预算下，我们的免训练方法在长序列理解和检索任务上表现出了在几个强基线上的优越性能，同时保持了语言建模的复杂性。

[NLP-147] FinTruthQA: A Benchmark Dataset for Evaluating the Quality of Financial Information Disclosure
[NLP-147] FinTruthQA：评估财务信息披露质量的基准数据集

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12009
作者: Ziyue Xu,Peilin Zhou,Xinyu Shi,Jiageng Wu,Yikang Jiang,Bin Ke,Jie Yang
关键词: Accurate and transparent, information disclosure, financial information disclosure, transparent financial information, information disclosure platforms
中文关键词: 准确透明、信息披露、财务信息披露、透明财务信息、信息披露平台
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Accurate and transparent financial information disclosure is crucial in the fields of accounting and finance, ensuring market efficiency and investor confidence. Among many information disclosure platforms, the Chinese stock exchanges’ investor interactive platform provides a novel and interactive way for listed firms to disclose information of interest to investors through an online question-and-answer (QA) format. However, it is common for listed firms to respond to questions with limited or no substantive information, and automatically evaluating the quality of financial information disclosure on large amounts of QA pairs is challenging. This paper builds a benchmark FinTruthQA, that can evaluate advanced natural language processing (NLP) techniques for the automatic quality assessment of information disclosure in financial QA data. FinTruthQA comprises 6,000 real-world financial QA entries and each QA was manually annotated based on four conceptual dimensions of accounting. We benchmarked various NLP techniques on FinTruthQA, including statistical machine learning models, pre-trained language model and their fine-tuned versions, as well as the large language model GPT-4. Experiments showed that existing NLP models have strong predictive ability for real question identification and question relevance tasks, but are suboptimal for answer relevance and answer readability tasks. By establishing this benchmark, we provide a robust foundation for the automatic evaluation of information disclosure, significantly enhancing the transparency and quality of financial reporting. FinTruthQA can be used by auditors, regulators, and financial analysts for real-time monitoring and data-driven decision-making, as well as by researchers for advanced studies in accounting and finance, ultimately fostering greater trust and efficiency in the financial markets.
摘要：准确、透明的财务信息披露是会计和金融领域确保市场效率和投资者信心的关键。在众多信息披露平台中，中国证券交易所的投资者互动平台为上市公司提供了一种新颖的互动方式，通过在线问答(QA)的形式披露投资者感兴趣的信息。然而，上市公司在回答问题时往往存在实质性信息有限或没有实质性信息的情况，对大量问答对的财务信息披露质量进行自动评估是具有挑战性的。本文构建了一个能够评估先进的自然语言处理(NLP)技术的基准FinTruthQA，用于自动评估金融QA数据中的信息披露质量。FinTruthQA包括6,000个真实的金融QA条目，每个QA都根据会计的四个概念维度进行了手动注释。我们在FinTruthQA上对各种NLP技术进行了基准测试，包括统计机器学习模型、预训练语言模型及其微调版本，以及大型语言模型GPT-4。实验表明，现有的NLP模型对真实问题识别和问题相关性任务具有较强的预测能力，但对答案相关性和可读性任务的预测能力较差。通过建立这一基准，我们为信息披露的自动评估提供了坚实的基础，大大提高了财务报告的透明度和质量。审计师、监管机构和金融分析师可以使用FinTruthQA进行实时监控和数据驱动的决策，研究人员也可以使用FinTruthQA进行会计和金融方面的高级研究，最终提高金融市场的信任度和效率。

[NLP-148] Dialogue Action Tokens: Steering Language Models in Goal-Directed Dialogue with a Multi-Turn Planner
[NLP-148] 对话行动代币：与多回合规划者在目标导向对话中引导语言模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.11978
作者: Kenneth Li,Yiming Wang,Fernanda Viégas,Martin Wattenberg
关键词: Dialogue Action Tokens, approach called Dialogue, plan goal-directed dialogues, Action Tokens, called Dialogue Action
中文关键词: 对话行动代币，称为对话的方法，计划目标导向的对话，行动代币，称为对话行动
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: Code: this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We present an approach called Dialogue Action Tokens (DAT) that adapts language model agents to plan goal-directed dialogues. The core idea is to treat each utterance as an action, thereby converting dialogues into games where existing approaches such as reinforcement learning can be applied. Specifically, we freeze a pretrained language model and train a small planner model that predicts a continuous action vector, used for controlled generation in each round. This design avoids the problem of language degradation under reward optimization. When evaluated on the Sotopia platform for social simulations, the DAT-steered LLaMA model surpasses GPT-4’s performance. We also apply DAT to steer an attacker language model in a novel multi-turn red-teaming setting, revealing a potential new attack surface.
摘要：我们提出了一种名为对话动作令牌（DART）的方法，该方法使语言模型代理能够规划目标导向的对话。核心思想是将每个话语视为一个动作，从而将对话转化为可以应用强化学习等现有方法的游戏。具体来说，我们冻结预先训练的语言模型并训练一个小型规划者模型，该模型预测连续动作载体，用于每轮的受控生成。该设计避免了奖励优化下语言退化的问题。当在Sotopia平台上进行社交模拟评估时，DT引导的LLaMA模型的性能优于GPT-4。我们还应用DART来在新颖的多回合红色团队设置中引导攻击者语言模型，揭示了潜在的新攻击表面。

[NLP-149] Reframing linguistic bootstrapping as joint inference using visually-grounded grammar induction models
[NLP-149] 使用基于视觉的语法归纳模型将语言引导重新构建为联合推理

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.11977
作者: Eva Portelance,Siva Reddy,Timothy J. O’Donnell
关键词: linguistic domain, posit that children, prior knowledge, syntactic bootstrapping posit, learning
中文关键词: 语言领域，孩子们，先验知识，语法引导，学习
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Semantic and syntactic bootstrapping posit that children use their prior knowledge of one linguistic domain, say syntactic relations, to help later acquire another, such as the meanings of new words. Empirical results supporting both theories may tempt us to believe that these are different learning strategies, where one may precede the other. Here, we argue that they are instead both contingent on a more general learning strategy for language acquisition: joint learning. Using a series of neural visually-grounded grammar induction models, we demonstrate that both syntactic and semantic bootstrapping effects are strongest when syntax and semantics are learnt simultaneously. Joint learning results in better grammar induction, realistic lexical category learning, and better interpretations of novel sentence and verb meanings. Joint learning makes language acquisition easier for learners by mutually constraining the hypotheses spaces for both syntax and semantics. Studying the dynamics of joint inference over many input sources and modalities represents an important new direction for language modeling and learning research in both cognitive sciences and AI, as it may help us explain how language can be acquired in more constrained learning settings.
摘要：语义和句法自举假设儿童使用他们对一个语言领域的先验知识，比如句法关系，来帮助他们后来习得另一个领域，比如新单词的意义。支持这两种理论的实验结果可能会让我们相信，这两种学习策略是不同的，其中一种可能先于另一种。在这里，我们认为，它们都取决于一种更普遍的语言习得策略：联合学习。使用一系列基于神经视觉的语法归纳模型，我们证明了当句法和语义同时学习时，句法和语义的自举效应都最强。联合学习的结果是更好的语法归纳，现实的词类学习，以及对新句子和动词意义的更好解释。联合学习通过相互约束句法和语义的假设空间，使学习者更容易获得语言。研究多个输入源和通道上的联合推理的动力学是认知科学和人工智能中语言建模和学习研究的一个重要新方向，因为它可以帮助我们解释语言是如何在更受限的学习环境中获得的。

[NLP-150] ranscoders Find Interpretable LLM Feature Circuits
[NLP-150] 分析师找到可解释的LLM特征电路

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.11944
作者: Jacob Dunefsky,Philippe Chlenski,Neel Nanda
关键词: finding sparse subgraphs, key goal, goal in mechanistic, mechanistic interpretability, circuit analysis
中文关键词: 寻找稀疏子图、关键目标、机械性目标、机械性可解释性、电路分析
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 28 pages, 6 figures, 3 tables, 2 algorithms. Under review

点击查看摘要

Abstract:A key goal in mechanistic interpretability is circuit analysis: finding sparse subgraphs of models corresponding to specific behaviors or capabilities. However, MLP sublayers make fine-grained circuit analysis on transformer-based language models difficult. In particular, interpretable features – such as those found by sparse autoencoders (SAEs) – are typically linear combinations of extremely many neurons, each with its own nonlinearity to account for. Circuit analysis in this setting thus either yields intractably large circuits or fails to disentangle local and global behavior. To address this we explore transcoders, which seek to faithfully approximate a densely activating MLP layer with a wider, sparsely-activating MLP layer. We successfully train transcoders on language models with 120M, 410M, and 1.4B parameters, and find them to perform at least on par with SAEs in terms of sparsity, faithfulness, and human-interpretability. We then introduce a novel method for using transcoders to perform weights-based circuit analysis through MLP sublayers. The resulting circuits neatly factorize into input-dependent and input-invariant terms. Finally, we apply transcoders to reverse-engineer unknown circuits in the model, and we obtain novel insights regarding the greater-than circuit in GPT2-small. Our results suggest that transcoders can prove effective in decomposing model computations involving MLPs into interpretable circuits. Code is available at this https URL.
摘要：机械可解释性的一个关键目标是电路分析：找到与特定行为或能力相对应的模型的稀疏子图。然而，MLP子层使得对基于变压器的语言模型的细粒度电路分析变得困难。特别是，可解释的特征–如稀疏自动编码器(SAE)发现的特征–通常是极多神经元的线性组合，每个神经元都有自己的非线性。因此，在这种情况下的电路分析要么产生难以处理的大电路，要么无法理清局部和全局行为。为了解决这个问题，我们探索了代码转换器，它寻求忠实地近似密集激活的MLP层和更宽的、稀疏激活的MLP层。我们成功地在120M、410M和1.4B参数的语言模型上训练了代码转换器，并发现他们在稀疏性、忠实性和人类可解释性方面至少可以与SAE平起平坐。然后，我们介绍了一种使用代码转换器通过MLP子层执行基于权重的电路分析的新方法。由此产生的电路巧妙地分解为输入相关项和输入不变项。最后，我们应用代码转换器对模型中的未知电路进行反向工程，得到了关于GPT2-Small中大于电路的新见解。我们的结果表明，代码转换器可以有效地将涉及MLP的模型计算分解为可解释的电路。代码可在此HTTPS URL上找到。

[NLP-151] From Crowdsourced Data to High-Quality Benchmarks: Arena-Hard and BenchBuilder Pipeline
[NLP-151] 从众包数据到高质量基准：Arena-Hard和BenchBuilder Pipeline

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.11939
作者: Tianle Li,Wei-Lin Chiang,Evan Frick,Lisa Dunlap,Tianhao Wu,Banghua Zhu,Joseph E. Gonzalez,Ion Stoica
关键词: rapid evolution, evolution of language, Chatbot Arena, prompts, LLM
中文关键词: 快速进化，语言进化，Chatbot Arena，提示，法学硕士
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The rapid evolution of language models has necessitated the development of more challenging benchmarks. Current static benchmarks often struggle to consistently distinguish between the capabilities of different models and fail to align with real-world user preferences. On the other hand, live crowd-sourced platforms like the Chatbot Arena collect a wide range of natural prompts and user feedback. However, these prompts vary in sophistication and the feedback cannot be applied offline to new models. In order to ensure that benchmarks keep up with the pace of LLM development, we address how one can evaluate benchmarks on their ability to confidently separate models and their alignment with human preference. Under these principles, we developed BenchBuilder, a living benchmark that filters high-quality prompts from live data sources to enable offline evaluation on fresh, challenging prompts. BenchBuilder identifies seven indicators of a high-quality prompt, such as the requirement for domain knowledge, and utilizes an LLM annotator to select a high-quality subset of prompts from various topic clusters. The LLM evaluation process employs an LLM judge to ensure a fully automated, high-quality, and constantly updating benchmark. We apply BenchBuilder on prompts from the Chatbot Arena to create Arena-Hard-Auto v0.1: 500 challenging user prompts from a wide range of tasks. Arena-Hard-Auto v0.1 offers 3x tighter confidence intervals than MT-Bench and achieves a state-of-the-art 89.1% agreement with human preference rankings, all at a cost of only 25 and without human labelers. The BenchBuilder pipeline enhances evaluation benchmarks and provides a valuable tool for developers, enabling them to extract high-quality benchmarks from extensive data with minimal effort.
摘要：语言模型的快速发展要求开发更具挑战性的基准。当前的静态基准通常难以一致地区分不同型号的功能，也无法与真实世界的用户偏好保持一致。另一方面，像聊天机器人Arena这样的现场众包平台收集了广泛的自然提示和用户反馈。然而，这些提示的复杂程度各不相同，反馈不能离线应用于新车型。为了确保基准跟上LLM发展的步伐，我们解决了如何评估基准的能力，即它们自信地分离模型的能力以及它们与人类偏好的一致性。在这些原则下，我们开发了BenchBuilder，这是一个活的基准，可以过滤来自实时数据源的高质量提示，以便对新鲜的、具有挑战性的提示进行离线评估。BenchBuilder确定了高质量提示的七个指标，如对领域知识的要求，并利用LLM注释器从各种主题集群中选择高质量的提示子集。法学院评估流程聘请了法学院评委，以确保完全自动化、高质量并不断更新的基准。我们将BenchBuilder应用于聊天机器人竞技场的提示，以创建竞技场-Hard-Auto v0.1：500具有挑战性的来自广泛任务的用户提示。Arena-Hard-Auto v0.1提供的置信度间隔是MT-BENCH的3倍，并与人类偏好排名达到最先进的89.1%的一致性，所有这些都只需25英镑，并且没有人工标签员。BenchBuilder管道增强了评估基准，并为开发人员提供了一个宝贵的工具，使他们能够以最小的努力从大量数据中提取高质量的基准。

[NLP-152] A Critical Study of What Code-LLMs (Do Not) Learn
[NLP-152] 对代码法学硕士（不）学习内容的批判性研究

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.11930
作者: Abhinav Anand,Shweta Verma,Krishna Narasimhan,Mira Mezini
关键词: Large Language Models, Large Language, Language Models trained, demonstrated impressive performance, Language Models
中文关键词: 大型语言模型，大型语言，经过培训的语言模型，表现出令人印象深刻的性能，语言模型
类目: oftware Engineering (cs.SE); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Language Models trained on code corpora (code-LLMs) have demonstrated impressive performance in various coding assistance tasks. However, despite their increased size and training dataset, code-LLMs still have limitations such as suggesting codes with syntactic errors, variable misuse etc. Some studies argue that code-LLMs perform well on coding tasks because they use self-attention and hidden representations to encode relations among input tokens. However, previous works have not studied what code properties are not encoded by code-LLMs. In this paper, we conduct a fine-grained analysis of attention maps and hidden representations of code-LLMs. Our study indicates that code-LLMs only encode relations among specific subsets of input tokens. Specifically, by categorizing input tokens into syntactic tokens and identifiers, we found that models encode relations among syntactic tokens and among identifiers, but they fail to encode relations between syntactic tokens and identifiers. We also found that fine-tuned models encode these relations poorly compared to their pre-trained counterparts. Additionally, larger models with billions of parameters encode significantly less information about code than models with only a few hundred million parameters.
摘要：在代码语料库(CODE-LLMS)上训练的大型语言模型在各种编码辅助任务中表现出令人印象深刻的性能。然而，尽管它们的规模和训练数据集都有所增加，但CODE-LLM仍然存在局限性，如建议存在句法错误的代码、变量误用等。一些研究认为，CODE-LLM在编码任务中表现良好，因为它们使用自我注意和隐藏表征来编码输入标记之间的关系。然而，以前的工作并没有研究哪些码性质不是用码-LLMS编码的。在本文中，我们对注意图和代码的隐藏表示-LLMS进行了细粒度的分析。我们的研究表明，代码-LLM只对输入标记的特定子集之间的关系进行编码。具体地说，通过将输入标记分类为句法标记和标识，我们发现模型对句法标记之间和标识之间的关系进行了编码，但没有对句法标记和标识之间的关系进行编码。我们还发现，与预先训练的模型相比，微调后的模型对这些关系的编码效果很差。此外，与只有几亿个参数的模型相比，具有数十亿个参数的较大模型编码的代码信息要少得多。

[NLP-153] DocCGen: Document-based Controlled Code Generation
[NLP-153] DocCGen：基于文档的受控代码生成

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.11925
作者: Sameer Pimparkhede,Mehant Kammakomati,Srikanth G. Tamilselvam,Prince Kumar,Ashok Pon Kumar,Pushpak Bhattacharyya
关键词: Recent developments show, Recent developments, Large Language Models, resource-rich general-purpose languages, Large Language
中文关键词: 最近的发展显示，最近的发展，大型语言模型，资源丰富的通用语言，大型语言
类目: oftware Engineering (cs.SE); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recent developments show that Large Language Models (LLMs) produce state-of-the-art performance on natural language (NL) to code generation for resource-rich general-purpose languages like C++, Java, and Python. However, their practical usage for structured domain-specific languages (DSLs) such as YAML, JSON is limited due to domain-specific schema, grammar, and customizations generally unseen by LLMs during pre-training. Efforts have been made to mitigate this challenge via in-context learning through relevant examples or by fine-tuning. However, it suffers from problems, such as limited DSL samples and prompt sensitivity but enterprises maintain good documentation of the DSLs. Therefore, we propose DocCGen, a framework that can leverage such rich knowledge by breaking the NL-to-Code generation task for structured code languages into a two-step process. First, it detects the correct libraries using the library documentation that best matches the NL query. Then, it utilizes schema rules extracted from the documentation of these libraries to constrain the decoding. We evaluate our framework for two complex structured languages, Ansible YAML and Bash command, consisting of two settings: Out-of-domain (OOD) and In-domain (ID). Our extensive experiments show that DocCGen consistently improves different-sized language models across all six evaluation metrics, reducing syntactic and semantic errors in structured code. We plan to open-source the datasets and code to motivate research in constrained code generation.
摘要：最近的发展表明，大型语言模型(LLM)在自然语言(NL)到资源丰富的通用语言(如C++、Java和Python)的代码生成方面具有最先进的性能。然而，它们对结构化领域特定语言(DSL)(如YAML、JSON)的实际使用受到限制，因为特定于领域的模式、语法和定制通常在预培训期间LLMS看不到。已经作出努力，通过相关实例的情景学习或微调来缓解这一挑战。然而，它也存在一些问题，例如DSL样本有限，灵敏度迅速，但企业保留了良好的DSL文档。因此，我们提出了DocCGen框架，它可以通过将结构化代码语言的自然语言到代码的生成任务分解为两个步骤来利用这些丰富的知识。首先，它使用与NL查询最匹配的库文档来检测正确的库。然后，它利用从这些库的文档中提取的模式规则来约束解码。我们针对两种复杂的结构化语言Ansible YAML和Bash命令评估了我们的框架，该框架包括两个设置：域外(OOD)和域内(ID)。我们广泛的实验表明，DocCGen在所有六个评估指标上一致地改进了不同大小的语言模型，减少了结构化代码中的语法和语义错误。我们计划将数据集和代码开源，以推动约束代码生成方面的研究。

[NLP-154] Explainable assessment of financial experts credibility by classifying social media forecasts and checking the predictions with actual market data
[NLP-154] 通过对社交媒体预测进行分类并将预测与实际市场数据进行检查，对金融专家可信度进行解释性评估

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.11924
作者: Silvia García-Méndez,Francisco de Arriba-Pérez,Jaime González-Gonzáleza,Francisco J. González-Castaño
关键词: include diverse interaction, media include diverse, Social media include, diverse interaction metrics, interaction metrics related
中文关键词: 包括多样化的互动、媒体包括多样化的、社交媒体包括、多样化的互动指标、相关的互动指标
